bio_p1: Born in Ukraine, Bogdan Nesterenko is a graduate of the Kharkiv State Conservatory (Ukraine) in accordion, orchestral conducting, and chamber music, and is a laureate of several International Accordion Competitions.
bio_p2: Based in France since 2006, Bogdan Nesterenko performs extensively across Europe in both solo and chamber music recitals. He is frequently invited to perform at prestigious venues (Vichy Opera, Villa Strauli in Switzerland, Théâtre National du Beauvaisis, Ebersmunster Abbey, Lille Opera, Luxembourg Philharmonic…) and renowned festivals (Lille Piano Festival, Baroque Music Festivals of Strasbourg and Madiran, International Organ Festival in Flanders, Septembre Musical de l'Orne, Nuit Blanche in Paris...) throughout Europe, Ukraine, and Asia. His performances can be heard on France Musique, BBC Radio, and CultureBox.
bio_p3: Bogdan Nesterenko performs with violinist Stefan Stalanowski (Super-Soloist of the Orchestre National de Lille), the GoYa Quartet, and the Elysée Quartet, as well as with pianist Alain Raës and violinist Pablo Schatzman, the Camerata de Flandre, and the vocal ensemble Métamorphoses. He formed the Trio SVITA with Benjamin and Khrystyna Boursier (violins) and performs with Jan Vermeire (organ), Dorian Gheorghilas (pan flute), and soprano Olga Vojnovic.
bio_p4: In the field of contemporary music, he has participated in premieres by M. Bourbon, Ch. Hache, N. Debard, J. Dassié, and S. Fache, and premiered the 'Concerto de deux mondes' (Concerto of Two Worlds) for accordion and symphony orchestra by Omar Yagoubi.
bio_p5: He also performs regularly with soprano Juliette de Massy. Their recording of Bach arias, 'Comme un air de passions...' (ed. AR-RE-SE), received critical acclaim. In a duo with Marc Hervieux (recorders), he recorded the album 'Dialogue insolite' (Unusual Dialogue) for RAINBOW CLASSIC. The musician's latest solo album, 'Un Sacre du printemps' (A Rite of Spring), is dedicated to Ukrainian and Russian music.
bio_p6: Bogdan Nesterenko plays a 'Jupiter' brand 'bayan' accordion, an instrument that, thanks to its tonal richness and multiple registers, offers a vast range of musical possibilities.
album1_description: Bogdan Nesterenko's latest album was recorded in his hometown of Kharkiv, Ukraine. The disc features an excerpt from 'The Rite of Spring' alongside transcriptions of major 19th-century Russian orchestral and piano masterpieces (including 'Pictures at an Exhibition', 'The Seasons', and 'The Nutcracker'). It also showcases original contemporary works for accordion by Ukrainian composers such as Runchak and Koukouzenko.
album2_description: A program of 18th-century German sonatas presented in a unique and unprecedented instrumental pairing. Historically, the recorder and accordion have never shared a common history or repertoire. Yet today, they meet in a program that brings together both celebrated and rare gems of the Baroque era. This album was recorded in October 2016 at the Church of the Shield (Église du Bouclier) in Strasbourg.
album3_description: Transcriptions of J.S. Bach's masterpieces for soprano and concert accordion. The album includes excerpts from the Passions and cantatas performed as a duo, as well as the Toccata and Fugue in D minor (BWV 565) and the Prelude and Fugue in A minor (BWV 543) for solo accordion.
album4_description: Recorded and released in Ukraine, Bogdan Nesterenko's second solo album features transcriptions of Baroque music (J.S. Bach, A. Vivaldi, T. Albinoni, D. Buxtehude) alongside original late 20th-century works for bayan (concert accordion) by Ukrainian composers A. Gaïdenko, A. Beloshitsky, and V. Zubitsky.
project_svita_desc_1: The Trio SVITA was born from an artistic and friendly encounter between three virtuoso musicians accustomed to European stages. The combination of two violins and the bayan creates an original sound palette, allowing them to explore a varied repertoire ranging from Bach to Piazzolla, including Slavic and Romani music. Their highly cohesive interpretation gives the impression of a single musical body driven by strong emotional intensity.
project_svita_desc_2: The choice of repertoire, marked by their Ukrainian roots — 'SVITA' meaning 'the light of the world' — brings particular depth to their performances. The trio captivates with the accessibility of its programs, the originality of the transcriptions, and the artistic commitment of the musicians.
